result.markdown: --- title: "[summaries.register_glance()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Register a `glance` adapter for a model class. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/summaries/_registry.py#L85-L117){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python summaries.register_glance(class_path, fn) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} This is the extension point for adding `glance()` support to new estimator classes. Each adapter is a callable that accepts a fitted model and returns a one-row summary DataFrame with model-level statistics (e.g., log-likelihood, AIC, number of observations). ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [class_path]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Fully qualified class name used as the registry key, e.g., `"greenwood._cox.CoxPH"`. [fn]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Tidier]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Callable with signature `fn(model, *, format=None, **kwargs) -> DataFrame`. ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} Register a custom glance adapter: ::: {#e54861bd .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} from greenwood.summaries import register_glance # Define a glance adapter that returns model-level statistics def _glance_my_model(model, *, format=None, **kwargs): from greenwood._backends import to_dataframe return to_dataframe({"n": [model.n_], "loglik": [model.loglik_]}, format=format) # Register the adapter for the custom model class register_glance("mypackage.MyModel", _glance_my_model) ``` :::
